Classification Report: Shrinking Villages in Aomori & Akita

Author: Julian | Primary metric: balanced_accuracy

1. Data Overview

  • Samples: 65
  • Features (primary experiment): 46
  • Classes: stable, shrinking, severely_shrinking
  • Class distribution: shrinking: 23, severely_shrinking: 22, stable: 20

2. Feature Leakage Analysis

Warning: Target (shrinkage_class) is derived from elderly_ratio thresholds. Features elderly_ratio, aging_index, youth_ratio are deterministic functions of the target and constitute data leakage.

Primary experiment: no_leaky (46 features)

ExperimentFeaturesDescription
all_features49All 30 features (reference — includes leaky features)
no_leaky46Drops elderly_ratio, aging_index, youth_ratio (PRIMARY)
no_demographic42Drops all demographic features
rs_only40Remote sensing features only

display_name balanced_accuracy accuracy f1_weighted f1_macro precision_weighted recall_weighted cohen_kappa matthews_corrcoef

4. Best Model: Gradient Boosting

balanced_accuracy=0.659, accuracy=0.662, f1_weighted=0.653, f1_macro=0.650, precision_weighted=0.688, recall_weighted=0.662, cohen_kappa=0.490, matthews_corrcoef=0.504

5. Feature Importance

6. Statistical Tests

Friedman test: χ²=140.31, p=0.0000

  • Friedman test significant (p=0.0000): classifiers differ in performance.
  • Gradient Boosting significantly outperforms Dummy (Most Frequent) (p=0.0000).
  • Gradient Boosting significantly outperforms Dummy (Stratified) (p=0.0000).

7. PCA Diagnostics

Components for 95% variance: 1

Generated by the classification pipeline. All results based on 5×5 Repeated Stratified K-Fold CV.
